method.step_1: Represent each truck-day with stop-zone, GPS image, route distance, and fleet metadata.
method.step_2: Build stop-profile vectors and route-image clusters as evidence.
method.step_3: Ask the LLM to reason over statistical evidence and produce final pairwise truck correlations.
method.step_4: Normalize positive LLM-reasoned target-to-other correlations into truck appearance frequencies.
method.step_5: Sample target route clusters by target route frequency and other-truck routes by joint frequency.
method.truck_appearance_frequency: Positive target-to-other LLM-reasoned correlations normalized across other trucks.
method.route_appearance_frequency: Cluster days divided by selected period length.
method.joint_scenario_frequency: LLM truck appearance frequency multiplied by route appearance frequency.

1247.llm_rationale: Strong stop-profile correlation (0.71) and moderate kms similarity (0.57) support mid-range correlation despite purpose mismatch (0.0). Duty-cycle match and reasonable age similarity stabilize the estimate.

llm_feature_interpretation.stop_profile_correlation: Strong (0.71) zone-pass pattern similarity indicates meaningful behavioral convergence in stop zones.
llm_feature_interpretation.route_similarity: Very weak (0.036) suggests different route structures despite shared stop-zone network.
llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Purpose mismatch (0.0) is primary downside; offset by duty-cycle match (1.0) and moderate kms similarity (0.57).

1311.llm_rationale: Negative stop-profile correlation (-0.20) dominates despite strong kms similarity (0.80) and duty-cycle match (1.0). Purpose mismatch (0.0) and weak route similarity (0.059) prevent recovery. Inverse behavioral patterns confirm low similarity.

llm_feature_interpretation.stop_profile_correlation: Negative (-0.20) zone-pass correlation indicates opposing stop-zone service patterns despite mileage alignment.
llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Strong kms similarity (0.80) contradicted by negative stop-profile (-0.20), suggesting trucks cover similar distances through fundamentally different stop-zone strategies.

1312.llm_rationale: Strong stop-profile correlation (0.71) and very strong kms similarity (0.80) offset purpose mismatch (0.0). Weak route similarity indicates similar operating intensity but distinct route patterns, with duty-cycle match stabilizing the correlation.

llm_feature_interpretation.stop_profile_correlation: Strong (0.71) zone-pass overlap suggests substantial alignment in stop-zone strategy despite purpose difference.
llm_feature_interpretation.route_similarity: Very weak (0.029) reflects minimal route-image similarity despite high daily-distance alignment.
llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Purpose mismatch (0.0) reduces correlation, but strong kms similarity (0.80) and duty-cycle match (1.0) suggest operational convergence in intensity and cycle type.

1350.llm_rationale: Moderate stop-profile correlation (0.57) with strong kms similarity (0.66) and perfect purpose/duty-cycle match. Weak route similarity suggests similar operating intensity but divergent route choices.

llm_feature_interpretation.stop_profile_correlation: Moderate zone-pass correlation (0.57) reflects partial overlap in stop zones.
llm_feature_interpretation.route_similarity: Very weak (0.046) despite high kms similarity, indicating similar daily distances served through different route structures.
llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Perfect purpose and duty-cycle alignment with comparable mileage profiles (0.66) suggest identical operational role.

1363.llm_rationale: Moderate-strong stop-profile correlation (0.64) is undermined by weak kms similarity (0.24), purpose mismatch (0.0), and very weak route similarity (0.036). Duty-cycle match provides minimal lift.

llm_feature_interpretation.stop_profile_correlation: Moderate-strong (0.64) zone-pass correlation suggests some behavioral alignment despite low mileage correlation.
llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Weak kms similarity (0.24) is anomalous for trucks with strong stop-profile (0.64), suggesting different daily distance budgets but overlapping stop-zone networks.

1421.llm_rationale: Moderate stop-profile correlation (0.49) with strong kms similarity (0.68) and perfect purpose/duty-cycle match. Lower stop-profile score suggests partial zone overlap despite similar purpose and mileage.

llm_feature_interpretation.stop_profile_correlation: Moderate (0.49) zone-pass correlation indicates partial overlap in stop-zone usage patterns.
llm_feature_interpretation.route_similarity: Very weak (0.045) despite kms similarity, suggesting different stop sequencing within similar distance budgets.
llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Perfect purpose and duty-cycle match with high kms similarity (0.68) indicate comparable operational scale but different stop-zone selection.

1423.llm_rationale: Negative stop-profile correlation (-0.20) dominates despite strong kms similarity (0.78), duty-cycle match (1.0), and weak route similarity (0.061). Purpose mismatch (0.0) compounds low similarity. Opposite behavioral patterns confirmed.

llm_feature_interpretation.stop_profile_correlation: Negative (-0.20) zone-pass correlation indicates opposing stop-zone service patterns.
llm_feature_interpretation.route_similarity: Very weak (0.061) reflects minimal route-cluster overlap.
llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Strong kms similarity (0.78) contradicted by negative stop-profile (-0.20), indicating trucks with similar daily distances but inverse stop-zone targeting.

1506.llm_rationale: Zero stop-profile correlation (0.0), purpose mismatch (0.0), and duty-cycle mismatch (0.0) produce minimal similarity. Weak route similarity (0.191, notably highest in dataset) and moderate kms similarity (0.34) insufficient to overcome structural misalignment.

llm_feature_interpretation.stop_profile_correlation: Zero (0.0) zone-pass correlation indicates no behavioral overlap in stop-zone usage.
llm_feature_interpretation.route_similarity: Weak (0.191) is highest in entire dataset, yet insufficient to drive meaningful similarity given zero stop-profile correlation.
llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Duty-cycle mismatch (0.0) is unique in dataset and dominant factor; combined with zero stop-profile (0.0) and purpose mismatch (0.0) confirms fundamental operational divergence.

1575.llm_rationale: Strong stop-profile correlation (0.65) combined with exceptional kms similarity (0.97) nearly offset purpose mismatch (0.0). Weak route similarity and moderate duty-cycle presence confirm route-structure divergence despite distance alignment.

llm_feature_interpretation.stop_profile_correlation: Strong (0.65) zone-pass overlap across weekday/weekend periods.
llm_feature_interpretation.route_similarity: Very weak (0.034) despite the highest kms similarity (0.97) in dataset, suggesting identical daily distances covered via entirely different routes.
llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Exceptional kms alignment (0.97) dominates; purpose mismatch (0.0) is primary limitation. Suggests similar duty cycles compensate for purpose difference.

1576.llm_rationale: Strong stop-profile correlation (0.63) and perfect purpose/duty-cycle match support the statistical score. Moderate kms similarity (0.51) and weak route similarity confirm mid-range behavioral alignment.

llm_feature_interpretation.stop_profile_correlation: Strong zone-pass pattern overlap (0.63) indicates similar stop-zone behavior across weekday/weekend.
llm_feature_interpretation.route_similarity: Very weak route-image similarity (0.048) suggests different route clusters despite zone overlap.
llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Perfect purpose match (1.0) and duty-cycle match (1.0) offset weak age similarity (0.28), indicating same truck type with different model years.

1578.llm_rationale: Strong stop-profile correlation (0.68) and very strong kms similarity (0.82) are dampened by purpose mismatch (0.0) and very weak route similarity (0.025). Duty-cycle match preserves mid-range correlation.

llm_feature_interpretation.stop_profile_correlation: Strong (0.68) zone-pass correlation indicates substantial alignment in stop-zone frequency and distribution.
llm_feature_interpretation.route_similarity: Very weak (0.025) is lowest in dataset, suggesting minimal route-cluster overlap despite stop-zone behavioral similarity.
llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Very strong kms similarity (0.82) offset by purpose mismatch (0.0); duty-cycle match (1.0) stabilizes overall estimate.

1667.llm_rationale: Zero stop-profile correlation (0.0) paired with purpose mismatch (0.0) yields low correlation. Moderate route similarity (0.073) and kms similarity (0.54) provide minimal lift. Duty-cycle match alone insufficient.

llm_feature_interpretation.stop_profile_correlation: Zero (0.0) zone-pass correlation indicates no detectable behavioral overlap in stop-zone patterns.
llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Both purpose mismatch (0.0) and zero stop-profile (0.0) combine to produce very low similarity despite duty-cycle match (1.0).

1687.llm_rationale: Moderate stop-profile correlation (0.45) with weak route similarity (0.083, higher than most) yields low-mid correlation. Purpose mismatch, weak kms similarity (0.31), and moderate age similarity further reduce similarity.

llm_feature_interpretation.stop_profile_correlation: Moderate (0.45) zone-pass overlap is below average; suggests partial stop-zone alignment only.
llm_feature_interpretation.route_similarity: Weak (0.083) is higher than many pairs, indicating some shared route structure despite weak stop-profile.
llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Purpose mismatch (0.0) and low kms similarity (0.31) dominate; duty-cycle match (1.0) insufficient to elevate correlation.

1688.llm_rationale: Negative stop-profile correlation (-0.12) severely undermines similarity despite perfect purpose/duty-cycle match (1.0). Higher-than-typical route similarity (0.120) cannot compensate. Trucks operate in opposite stop-zone patterns.

llm_feature_interpretation.stop_profile_correlation: Negative (-0.12) zone-pass correlation indicates trucks serve substantially different or inverse stop-zone distributions.
llm_feature_interpretation.route_similarity: Weak but notable (0.120), highest in dataset, suggesting route structure overlap despite zone-pattern inversion.
llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Perfect purpose (1.0) and duty-cycle match (1.0) are contradicted by negative stop-profile correlation, indicating same truck type with fundamentally different operational patterns.

1785.llm_rationale: Negative stop-profile correlation (-0.17) dominates despite perfect purpose/duty-cycle match (1.0) and moderate kms similarity (0.52). Trucks exhibit inverse zone-pass behavior, negating purpose alignment.

llm_feature_interpretation.stop_profile_correlation: Negative (-0.17) zone-pass correlation indicates opposite or highly divergent stop-zone service patterns.
llm_feature_interpretation.route_similarity: Very weak (0.038) confirms minimal structural overlap.
llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Despite perfect purpose/duty-cycle match, negative stop-profile correlation indicates trucks serve different operational niches despite same type classification.

1993.llm_rationale: Exceptionally strong stop-profile correlation (0.86) dominates despite purpose mismatch (0.0). Duty-cycle match preserves similarity despite different truck purpose, suggesting zone-based behavioral convergence overrides purpose differences.

llm_feature_interpretation.stop_profile_correlation: Very strong (0.86) zone-pass overlap indicates trucks serve similar stop-zone networks regardless of declared purpose.
llm_feature_interpretation.route_similarity: Weak (0.051) confirms different route structures within same stop zones.
llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Purpose mismatch (0.0) and lower kms similarity (0.37) are offset by strong duty-cycle match (1.0), suggesting operational behavior transcends purpose classification.

2191.llm_rationale: Moderate stop-profile correlation (0.63) combined with strong kms similarity (0.82) is dampened by purpose mismatch (0.0). Weak route similarity and moderate age similarity yield low-to-mid-range correlation.

llm_feature_interpretation.route_similarity: Very weak (0.035); minimal route structure overlap.
llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Strong kms similarity (0.82) partially offsets purpose mismatch (0.0). Duty-cycle match (1.0) preserves mid-range estimate.

2201.llm_rationale: Strong stop-profile correlation (0.74) and higher-than-average route similarity (0.082) are tempered by purpose mismatch (0.0) and lower kms similarity (0.33). Duty-cycle match provides stability.

llm_feature_interpretation.stop_profile_correlation: Strong (0.74) zone-pass correlation indicates robust stop-zone behavioral alignment.
llm_feature_interpretation.route_similarity: Weak but notable (0.082), higher than most pairs, suggesting some shared route structures.
llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Purpose mismatch (0.0) and lower kms similarity (0.33) reduce correlation; strong stop-profile (0.74) and duty-cycle match (1.0) maintain mid-range estimate.

2518.llm_rationale: Strong stop-profile correlation (0.74) offset by purpose mismatch (0.0), weak route similarity (0.034), and lower kms similarity (0.39). Duty-cycle match and moderate age similarity maintain threshold correlation.

llm_feature_interpretation.stop_profile_correlation: Strong (0.74) zone-pass alignment indicates similar stop-zone service patterns.
llm_feature_interpretation.route_similarity: Very weak (0.034); minimal route-cluster overlap despite strong stop-zone correlation.
llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Purpose mismatch (0.0) and lower kms (0.39) are primary limiting factors; strong stop-profile (0.74) prevents drop below 0.50.

final_scenario.sampling_method: For each scenario day, sample the target truck route from its own route_appearance_frequency, then sample one other truck route from joint_scenario_frequency = truck_appearance_frequency * route_appearance_frequency.
